Empires & Puzzles Belladonna Review: The Witch of Ward and Wellness

Is Belladonna's unique dispel immunity enough to save her from the dustbin of history? We dive deep into this Bard family healer to see if her potions still pack a punch in today's high-stat meta or if she's just another relic of the past.
Belladonna Review: The Witch of Ward and Wellness
Belladonna: The Witch of Ward and Wellness
Belladonna is a 5-star Dark (Purple) healer from the

, originally released during the Challenge Festival II in March 2023. Depicted as a somewhat hag-like potion brewer with an electric cello (a bard's got to play, right?), she brings a mix of healing, offensive buffing, and protection to the table. In her prime, she was a highly sought-after support hero. Today, she stands as a "classic" example of excellent utility fighting against the relentless tide of stat inflation.

Belladonna embraces the "wicked witch" aesthetic but with a musical twist. She sports a classic pointed hat, a mischievous grin, and a basket full of questionable potions (and eyeballs). The most striking element is her instrument—a cello that seems to be powered by purple magic rather than electricity. It’s a character design that screams "I will heal you, but I might turn you into a newt if you complain."
When we look at Belladonna's stats, we have to address the elephant in the room:
Her base stats are significantly lower than modern 5-star heroes. Even with a second Limit Break (LB2), her stats (approx. 1059/1049/1915) barely scratch the surface of what a modern hero has
. She is incredibly fragile by today's standards and will struggle to survive against the massive damage output of current snipers and AoE hitters.
, is a Swiss Army knife of support.
A solid heal at Average speed. It's reliable, though not an overheal (boosted health), which is a slight drawback in the modern era of max-health reduction.
This is a unique mechanic. Instead of just removing fiends, she turns them into fuel for
healing. If your team is covered in fiends, she heals more effectively, helping to clear those parasitic pests and top up your team in one go.
A massive attack boost for all allies. This is excellent for turning the tide of battle and crushing the enemy team with tile damage or specials.

The crown jewel. Making your buffs (including her own massive attack up) undispellable for 4 turns is huge. Thanks to updates (specifically v61), this also protects against "stealing" and "reallocation" of buffs, shutting down heroes like The Hatter or Ithar.

With 3 Fiends per ally (e.g., vs Elizabeth): 42% + 24% = 66% Healing.
4 Turns of guaranteed +54% Attack. This essentially guarantees high damage output for your team unless the enemy kills you outright.

This passive targets heroes who gain mana via Special Skills (e.g., Xnolphod, Grimble). While less critical in today's meta compared to when she was released, it still provides a nice layer of disruption against mana-boosting teams.
Note: The exact percentage has been debated between 50% and 80% in various data sources, but practically, it significantly cripples mana gain.
At the start of battle, she gets a defense buff. Given her outdated stats, this is arguably the best Aether Power she could ask for to help her survive the opening turns.
Upon release, Belladonna was a top-tier support. Her high attack buff combined with dispel immunity made her a staple in war attacks and a solid defensive flank.
The stat gap is undeniable. She dies easily. A modern sniper can likely one-shot her even through her Aether defense buff.
She is no longer a top-tier defensive option. However, on

, she is still a playmaker. Her ability to lock in a +54% attack buff is invaluable for Titans and specific war attacks where you can protect her. She has transitioned from a "Must-Have" to a "Strategic Tool."

. If you fire Belladonna first, then Ludwig, the enemy cannot dispel the Taunt, forcing them to hit Ludwig while your team charges up.

. Even with "low" stats for veterans, for a new player, a 5-star healer who gives +54% attack is a game-changer. She will carry you through map stages and early raids.
She is likely a "bench" hero for your main war defense. Do

use Alpha Aethers (LB2) on her unless you use her daily for Titans or have a specific war attack team that relies on her dispel immunity. There are simply better, sturdier clerics/healers available now (like the Toon costumes or recent Goblins).
Upon release, there was confusion about whether she healed for each enemy fiend or ally fiend. It turned out to be ally fiends, leading to comments like,
"Just remove the fiends before healing... no need to introduce a new mechanic for absolutely no reason."
Players immediately recognized her value against dispellers.
"The immunity to buff dispel... me likey."
Many expected a beautiful "Belladonna" and got a witch.
"Is this Boldtusk’s older brother who went to the dark side to grow dope and play bass in a doom metal band?"
Belladonna is a hero who has aged gracefully in utility, if not in physique. Her stats may be creaky and arthritic compared to the young whippersnappers of the Astral and Construct families, but her
still plays a tune that can win games. The combination of a massive attack buff and the ability to say "No" to dispellers ensures she remains a valuable tool in any tactician's roster.
A fragile but potent support. Keep her safe, and she'll make your team unstoppable.
A comprehensive review of Belladonna, the 5-star Dark healer from the Bard family. Analyze her unique dispel immunity, fiend counter mechanics, and modern viability in the current meta.
